# Raphael J. Edwards

> Technology Executive & Services Architect based in Boston, MA. Motto: "Building resilient teams. Solving complex problems." Contact: raphaeledwards@gmail.com

## About

Raphael J. Edwards is a technology executive with a "builder" mindset who architects strategic programs that scale global success. With deep roots in cloud security, enterprise growth, and engineering leadership, he leads high-performance teams that deliver measurable business value — turning small investments into multi-million dollar recurring revenue loops.

His core leadership philosophy: technology is easy, people are hard. He advocates for blameless post-mortems, psychological safety, and OKR frameworks to move engineering organizations from chaos to clarity. He believes in orchestrating hybrid human-AI workflows where AI amplifies human creativity rather than replacing it.

He is a father to twin boys, rides gravel bikes in the Fells, appreciates mechanical watches and manual transmissions, and runs on coffee and lo-fi beats.

## Expertise

- **Team Strategy & Organizational Scaling:** Building high-performance cultures, embedding OKR frameworks, coaching global teams across NA, Costa Rica, and India. Directed a 45-person global services organization.
- **Cybersecurity & Edge Security:** Zero Trust architecture, cloud-native security, Edge protection, IoT security. Believes Zero Trust is a culture, not a product.
- **Cloud Architecture:** Resilient, scalable infrastructure on AWS, GCP, and Azure. FinOps, microservices, cloud-native transformation.
- **AI & Future Tech:** AI agents in DevOps, connected vehicle OTA frameworks, autonomous systems, human-AI workflow orchestration.
- **Revenue & Services Strategy:** Services incentive programs, Value Management Offices (VMO), QBR delivery, go-to-market architecture.

## Key Achievements

- Directed a $70M+ ARR services organization serving as the primary enablement engine for a $300M+ Financial Institution portfolio
- Built and aligned a 45-person global team using OKR frameworks, consistently delivering on ambitious corporate goals
- Architected a services sales incentive plan that turned a $145k investment into $25M+ in new Annual Recurring Revenue
- Established an oversight committee that generated a 210% increase in new revenue by capturing service over-utilization
- Built a global Value Management Office (VMO) from ideation to operationalization, saving 2,300+ field hours in its first two years
- Established a global Blameless Post-Mortem program reaching 500+ services personnel, driving direct product enhancements
- Architected a secure Over-The-Air (OTA) delivery framework supporting 1M+ connected vehicles

## Recognition

- Global Services Pinnacle Award, 2023
- Akamai Leadership Academy, 2020
- Akamai Leadership Preparatory School, 2017
- Akamai Certified Instructor, 2016–2018
- Akamai "Titan" Award, 2015 — given annually to top performers across Global Sales, Services & Marketing

## Education

- B.S.B.A., Management Information Systems & Business Administration — University of Massachusetts Lowell, 2001–2005
- Project Management Professional (PMP) #1276180 — June 2009 to June 2024 (lapsed)
- AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner — February 2020 to February 2023

## Projects

- **Connected Vehicle Architecture** — Architected the secure Over-The-Air (OTA) delivery framework supporting 1M+ connected vehicles. Focus: IoT, firmware, cloud, edge security.
- **Secure Financial Transformation** — Directed the $70M+ services portfolio securing critical cloud workloads for top financial institutions. Focus: Zero Trust, compliance, banking.
- **Operational Intelligence (VMO)** — Built a Value Management Office leveraging data to save 2,300+ field hours globally. Focus: operations, automation, strategy.
- **Strategic Revenue Architecture** — Architected an incentive program turning a $145k investment into $25M+ in Annual Recurring Revenue. Focus: sales strategy, GTM, ARR growth.
- **Resilient Engineering Culture** — Established a Blameless Post-Mortem program for 500+ staff to drive continuous improvement. Focus: culture, leadership, agile.
- **Global Investment Strategy** — Led a global growth program converting a $1M investment into $28.8M in new annual recurring revenue. Focus: global scale, revenue, strategy.

## Writing

- **The Human Element in Digital Transformation** (Oct 2025) — Why technology is only 20% of the challenge. The real blocker is human resistance to change. Success requires internal champions, safe spaces for failure, and cultural renewal before the first line of new code.
- **Navigating the Edge: Security Implications** (Sep 2025) — The traditional security perimeter is dead. Zero Trust at the edge means identity verification for every device and workload, with observability and security baked into CI/CD.
- **Leadership in the Age of AI** (Aug 2025) — Managing hybrid human-AI teams requires moving up the abstraction ladder. Engineers must focus on architecture and empathy while guarding against knowledge gaps from over-reliance on generated code.
- **AI Agents in DevOps: The End of Pipelines?** (Nov 2025) — Static CI/CD is becoming obsolete. The future is Guardrail Engineering — defining boundaries and letting autonomous agents navigate safely.
- **Zero Trust isn't Product, It's Culture** (Nov 2025) — Zero Trust begins with "We are already breached." Every service must authenticate every request. The friction is intentional and makes software resilient by default.
- **Cloud FinOps: Your AWS Bill is Bleeding** (Nov 2025) — 40% of typical enterprise cloud spend is waste. Engineers need economic observability — cost visibility alongside latency and error rates.
- **Stop Measuring Lines of Code** (Dec 2025) — Real velocity is Cycle Time and DORA metrics: Deployment Frequency, Lead Time, Time to Restore, Change Failure Rate. Measure the system, not the worker.
- **Technical Debt as a Feature** (Dec 2025) — Debt is a leverage tool, not a moral failing. The problem is lack of a repayment plan. Allocate 20% of every sprint to paying it down.

## This Site

This portfolio is an AI-native interactive experience. Visitors can chat with a digital twin of Raphael powered by a RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) system using Google Gemini, grounded in Raphael's actual resume, projects, and writing. The site is built with React, Vite, Firebase, and Tailwind CSS and deployed on Vercel.
